Estimate: Green-water mismatch +0.033; antecedent retention +0.064; ventilation obstruction +0.054 probability units per 1 s.d.
Interpretation: All three pathway controls are positively associated with failure probability in the stress-conditioned sample.
Anthropogenic heat adds a smaller but positive association in the covered dense-city subset.
Predictive discrimination is modest but stable out of time.
Performance is regionally mixed, so the pathway model is interpreted mechanistically rather than as a universal forecast.

Estimate: Water mismatch: 0.035 lower probability and 4.40 billion represented resident-8-day intervals; retention: 0.027 and 1.35 billion; obstruction: 0.035 and 2.45 billion
Interpretation: Association-based scale linked to more water-supported, lower-retention and less-obstructed profiles.

Estimate: 0.098 lower probability; 8.40 billion represented resident-8-day intervals
Sample: Water mismatch, retention and obstruction standardized jointly
Interpretation: Upper-envelope association under the fitted pathway model, not a direct intervention effect.

Estimate: Adjusted failure probability 0.415 under high water support versus 0.526 under low water support; observed shares 44.6% versus 55.4%
Sample: 41,827 high-green-support DHD true-hot intervals in lower/upper regional water-support tertiles
Interpretation: Water-supported high-green systems show attenuated risk; this is within-system conditioning evidence, not an intervention estimate.
